title: Veterans Access to Direct Primary Care Act
short_title: Veterans Access to Direct Primary Care Act
plain_summary: This bill starts a five-year test program that lets some veterans get their regular doctor visits from doctors outside the Veterans Affairs system instead. Veterans who join the program will get money put into special health savings accounts to pay for this outside care. While they are in the program, they cannot get the same basic doctor services from Veterans Affairs. The program is only for veterans who are already signed up for VA health care.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Health.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Veterans Access to Direct Primary Care Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Pilot Program on Establishment of Veteran Health Savings Accounts to Allow Veterans to Access Direct Primary Care Service Arrangements
sections.text: The Department of Veterans Affairs will start a five year test program one year after this law is passed. In this test program, veterans who qualify can choose to get primary care from doctors who do not work for the Veterans Affairs Department. The veterans will get money put into a special health savings account to pay for this care. This test program will be run by the Center for Innovation for Care and Payment at Veterans Affairs. To qualify for this test program, a veteran must already be signed up to get medical care through the Veterans Affairs patient system. Veterans who join this test program will get a health savings account that can be used to buy primary care services from non Veterans Affairs doctors. The account money can also be used to pay for related costs like monthly fees to doctors for medical services, payments for medical services that check for or treat diseases and keep people healthy, and prescription medicines or drugs you can buy without a prescription. If a veteran chooses to get this health savings account, they cannot get the same type of primary care from Veterans Affairs doctors while they are in the test program. Veterans Affairs will create a system to stop people from cheating or using the health savings accounts in ways that are not allowed. Veterans Affairs will figure out how much money to put in each health savings account each year. They will work with experts who study insurance costs to decide this amount. Each veteran in the program will get this money put in their account once a year while they are in the test program. A direct primary care service arrangement means an agreement where a doctor provides a specific set of medical services to a patient for fixed monthly or yearly fees. Veterans Affairs must send reports to Congress about how the test program is working. During the first two years after this law is passed, they must send a report every three months about setting up the program. One of these reports must explain the final plan for how the test program will work. Starting one year after the last setup report is sent, Veterans Affairs must send a report once a year about how well the test program is working. The money needed to run this test program will come from money that Veterans Affairs already has for health care. It will not need extra money from Congress. Veterans Affairs can only put money into these health savings accounts for five years after this law is passed. After that, the program ends.
